elements.text:
Dutch elm disease: Introduced in the USA around 1928.
To the 60s: Spray applications (organochlorines) against vector
In the 70s: Lignasan BLP (carbendazim phosphate) injected into the base of the tree - was never especially effective. It is still sold under the name "Elm Fungicide".
Arbotect (thiabendazole hypophosphite) has been proven effective. Arbotect must be injected every two to three years; the disease generally cannot be eradicated once a tree is infected. 
Alamo (propiconazole) effective for only the current season. Alamo is primarily recommended for treatment of oak wilt

Coconut and oil palms: Trunk injection of systemic organophospate insecticides.
Tree Injection in the first half of XX centuryInjection of organic crop protection products
